Project Charter.Executive Summary: The Philadelphia Police Department (PPD) aims to develop and implement a department-wide community engagement framework to enhance trust and collaboration with the community. This project will establish clear standard operating procedures and ongoing support for district-level engagement, aligning with the PPD's strategic goal of advancing public safety through community partnerships.

Strategic Rationale.Objective: Develop and implement a department-wide community engagement framework.
Strategic Rationale.Company Goal: Advance Trust with the Community and Partners

Project Charter.Project Purpose: To create a structured approach for community engagement that fosters trust, transparency, and collaboration between the PPD and the communities it serves.
Strategic Objectives: Establish a comprehensive community engagement framework.
Create clear standard operating procedures for community policing.
Provide ongoing support and guidance to district-level community engagement initiatives.
Key Deliverables: Standard operating procedures for community policing.
Training materials for district-level officers on community engagement.
High-Level Requirements: Engagement of community stakeholders in the framework development.
Training sessions for officers on new procedures.
Regular feedback mechanisms from community members.
Project Constraints: Limited resources for training and implementation.
Potential resistance to change from within the department.
Need for alignment with existing departmental policies.
Project Assumptions: Community members will actively participate in the engagement process.
Officers will be receptive to new training and procedures.
Sufficient funding will be allocated for project implementation.

Schedule - Milestones.Milestone: Completion of Community Engagement Framework Draft

Success Criteria: Increased community participation in engagement activities.
Positive feedback from community surveys regarding trust and transparency.
Successful implementation of standard operating procedures across all districts.
High-Level Risks: Insufficient community engagement leading to ineffective procedures.
Budget constraints impacting training and implementation.
Potential pushback from officers resistant to new engagement strategies.
